Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class EmulatorStateProvider

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Reactive provider for emulator execution state, using a BehaviorSubject to publish state changes.

public sealed class EmulatorStateProvider : IEmulatorState
Inheritance
object
EmulatorStateProvider
Implements
IEmulatorState
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Implementation: Uses System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> to maintain the current state and broadcast updates to all subscribers. The BehaviorSubject guarantees that new subscribers immediately receive the most recent state.

Thread Safety: BehaviorSubject is thread-safe for publishing and subscribing. Multiple threads can safely call Update(StateSnapshot) and subscribe to Stream.

Initial State: Initialized with a default snapshot (PC=0, SP=0, Cycles=0, not running).

Control Methods: RequestPause(), RequestContinue(), and RequestStep() are currently placeholders. These will be implemented when the execution control loop is integrated with this provider.

Properties

Stream

Observable stream of emulator state snapshots.

public IObservable<StateSnapshot> Stream { get; }

Property Value

IObservable<StateSnapshot>

Remarks

Uses a System.Reactive.Subjects.BehaviorSubject<T> which guarantees that new subscribers immediately receive the most recent state snapshot, then receive all subsequent updates.

Methods

GetCurrent()

Gets the current emulator state snapshot.

public StateSnapshot GetCurrent()

Returns

StateSnapshot

Remarks

Returns the current value of the BehaviorSubject (most recently published snapshot). This is a synchronous operation that does not involve subscribing to the stream.

RequestContinue()

Requests the emulator to continue execution.

public void RequestContinue()

Remarks

TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator's execution controller to resume execution from the paused state.

RequestPause()

Requests the emulator to pause execution.

public void RequestPause()

Remarks

TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator's execution controller (likely via CancellationToken or state flag) to pause execution at the next safe point (typically after completing the current instruction).

RequestStep()

Requests the emulator to execute a single instruction step.

public void RequestStep()

Remarks

TODO: This is a placeholder. Implementation will signal the emulator to execute exactly one instruction, then pause again. Used for debugging and single-stepping through code.

Update(StateSnapshot)

Updates the emulator state with a new snapshot.

public void Update(StateSnapshot snapshot)

Parameters

snapshot StateSnapshot

Remarks

Publishes the new snapshot to all active subscribers via Stream. This method is typically called by the emulator's main execution loop after each instruction or at regular intervals.
